editor_workspace_overview.label: Understanding the editor, Run button, and terminal
editor_workspace_overview.summary: A quick tour of the workspace, then a short hands-on exercise to run code and verify output in the terminal.

intro.title: Your Workspace: Where You’ll Write and Run Code
intro.alt: {appName} code workspace showing the module sidebar, code editor, run button, and terminal output.
intro.caption:
**Quick tour**

- **Left:** Module sidebar (navigate sections/topics)
- **Center:** Code editor (where you write Python)
- **Top-right:** **Run** button (executes your code)
- **Bottom:** Terminal / console (shows output + errors)

instructions_intro.title: Input, Processing, Output (Using Your Workspace)
instructions_intro.bodyMarkdown:
Before we go further, I want to take a moment to make you familiar with your workspace.

> **Note:** These instructions are written for **desktop** users. If you’re on a **phone or tablet**, the layout may look a little different.

Let’s start on the **left side**: this is your **module sidebar**. You can navigate between sections by selecting one of the tiles.

On the **right**, you have your **code editor**.

Now take a look at the layout. Click the **Run** button (the triangle icon) in the **top-right corner** of your code editor and confirm the text inside the quotes is printed in the terminal.

Congratulations — you just learned how to execute your code and display the output in the terminal.

Now copy this code, erase the current one, and run it again:

~~~python
print("I am learning Python")
~~~

Verify that it also displays in the terminal.

When you finish, move on to the next topic and make sure you mark everything as read.

m0_workspace_run_button.title: Workspace: Run button
m0_workspace_run_button.prompt: What does the **Run** button do in your workspace?

options.a: Deletes your code so you can start over
options.b: Executes your code and shows results in the terminal

m0_workspace_run_button.hint: Run executes code; output appears in the terminal.

m0_workspace_terminal_output.title: Workspace: Terminal output
m0_workspace_terminal_output.prompt: Where do **print() output** and **errors** usually appear?
m0_workspace_terminal_output.hint: The terminal shows printed output and error messages.

m0_workspace_editor_area.title: Workspace: Code editor
m0_workspace_editor_area.prompt: Where do you type Python code before running it?
m0_workspace_editor_area.hint: You write code in the editor, then run it.
